questions.stem: A product manager wants to measure whether their customer support agent is improving after a series of prompt refinements. Which Agent Platform evaluation approach provides the most reliable before-and-after comparison?

options.text: Manually test a few conversations and compare the responses
Create an eval set with personas and scenarios, run it against a baseline version, then run the same eval set after changes and compare scores using regression detection
Monitor production analytics and wait for customer feedback to change

questions.explanation: The eval framework supports regression detection by comparing new runs against a baseline. Running the same eval set (same personas, scenarios, and evaluators) before and after changes gives a controlled comparison. The platform flags regressions when scores drop beyond a configurable threshold, showing exactly which persona/scenario combinations degraded.

questions.stem: A security team asks: 'How do we prevent our agent from leaking customer Social Security numbers in its responses?' Which Agent Platform safety layer is most directly designed for this?

options.text: Business constraints with REQUIRE conditions in the agent definition
An output guardrail with PII pattern detection that automatically redacts SSN-like patterns before the response reaches the user
An input guardrail that blocks users from entering SSNs

questions.explanation: Output guardrails with kind: output inspect the agent's generated response before it reaches the user. A PII pattern guardrail with action: redact automatically detects and removes SSN-like patterns from responses. Input guardrails would help prevent users from sharing SSNs, but the question specifically asks about preventing the agent from leaking them. Business constraints enforce business rules, not content filtering.

questions.stem: In the Agent Platform evaluation framework, the system executes the ___ product of all selected personas, scenarios, and variants, then scores each conversation with every selected evaluator.
questions.explanation: An eval set runs the Cartesian product: every persona talks through every scenario, repeated for the configured number of variants. For example, 3 personas, 4 scenarios, and 2 variants produce 24 conversations. Each conversation is then scored by every selected evaluator, creating a comprehensive test matrix.

questions.stem: A compliance officer wants to ensure agents in a healthcare project cannot provide medical diagnoses, even if users ask directly. They also want to detect prompt injection attempts. Which guardrail strategy is recommended?

options.text: Add a single output guardrail that blocks all medical terminology

Layer multiple guardrails: an input guardrail using an LLM check to detect off-topic medical diagnosis requests, an output guardrail for medical advice filtering, and a Lakera Guard provider for prompt injection detection
Rely solely on the agent's LIMITATIONS section to refuse medical questions

questions.explanation: The platform supports layered defense with multiple guardrails at different priority levels. Input guardrails catch prompt injection and off-topic requests before the LLM processes them. Output guardrails filter responses. Specialized providers like Lakera Guard detect sophisticated prompt injection. Relying only on LIMITATIONS depends on the LLM following instructions, which is not reliable against adversarial input.

questions.stem: An evaluation run completes with a high average score (4.2/5) but a high standard deviation (1.4). What does this pattern indicate, and what action should the team take?

options.text: The agent performs consistently well; deploy to production immediately
The agent is good on average but unpredictable; investigate the low-scoring outlier conversations to find specific failing scenarios
The evaluator rubric is too lenient and needs to be rewritten

questions.explanation: High average with high standard deviation means the agent performs well in most cases but fails badly in some. The platform's eval results let you drill into individual conversations to see full transcripts, evaluator reasoning, and trace timelines. The team should focus on understanding why specific persona/scenario combinations scored low and fix those edge cases before deployment.
